data.title: U.S. January Business Inventories Decline by 0.1%, Missing Expectations
data.description: According to Jin10, the U.S. business inventories for January decreased by 0.1%, falling short of the anticipated 0.1% increase. The previous month's data showed a 0.10% rise. This decline in inventories may indicate a shift in business strategies or consumer demand, impacting economic forecasts and market expectations.

data.title: U.S. ISM Manufacturing Prices Index Rises in March
data.description: The U.S. ISM Manufacturing Prices Index for March increased to 78.3, surpassing expectations of 73 and the previous value of 70.5. According to Jin10, this rise indicates growing inflationary pressures within the manufacturing sector, as manufacturers face higher costs for raw materials and components. The increase in the index suggests that price pressures are intensifying, which could impact production costs and consumer prices in the coming months. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments as they assess the potential implications for the broader economy and monetary policy.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Introduces Execution Tickets to Address PBS Monopoly
data.description:
Barnabé Monnot, head of the Robust Incentives Group (RIG) at the Ethereum Foundation, unveiled the 'Execution Tickets' mechanism at the EthCC[9] conference. According to Foresight News, Monnot highlighted that the current PBS architecture grants excessive monopoly power to block proposers. Execution Tickets aim to auction the execution and ordering rights of transactions within blocks to ticket holders at the protocol level, leaving validators with only signature and consensus functions, thus removing their direct involvement in MEV distribution.

Economically, the revenue from selling Execution Tickets will be directly burned by the protocol, similar to the EIP-1559 mechanism. This approach redistributes MEV profits, originally concentrated among a few high-level participants, in a deflationary manner to all ETH holders. For validators, the interception of MEV revenue by the ticket market will lead to smoother and more predictable income, eliminating the need for complex MEV-Boost plugins or the pursuit of hardware and network advantages, significantly lowering the participation threshold for home stakers. Monnot emphasized that Execution Tickets are a core proposal in Ethereum's roadmap phase 'The Scourge,' which, along with inclusion lists and other mechanisms, will effectively prevent specific builders from censoring transactions.

data.title: Oil Prices Remain High Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Conflict
data.description: Hopes for a de-escalation in the Iran conflict are encouraging, but tensions remain elevated, and navigation through the Strait of Hormuz is still restricted. According to Odaily, Forex.com market analyst Fawad Razaqzada noted that while crude oil prices have retreated, Brent crude futures are holding around $100 per barrel, indicating that the market is not yet fully convinced. Without a clearer timeline for reopening and, more importantly, a lack of credibility, it is difficult to foresee oil prices consistently falling below $100.

data.title: Coinbase: The MEZO-USD trading pair will open later today.
data.description: Coinbase announced that spot trading of Mezo (MEZO) will launch on April 1, 2026. If liquidity conditions are met, the MEZO-USD trading pair will begin later today and will be available in regions where trading is supported.

data.title: eToro opens crypto trading in New York State, service now available in 48 US states.
data.description: Social trading platform eToro announced that New York State residents can now trade crypto assets on its platform, trading in sync with stocks, ETFs, and options. With its New York State BitLicense and remittance license, eToro's crypto services now cover 48 states, complying with the strictest regulatory frameworks in the US. The platform offers social investing and educational tools, accessible to over 9 million New York users; data shows that 36% of US retail investors hold crypto assets, and 17% plan to increase their holdings. eToro stated that this move will expand digital financial inclusion and advance its mission of opening up global markets. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Analysts: With the Strait of Hormuz's navigation status uncertain, it's difficult to see oil prices continue to fall below $100.
data.description: Market hopes for a de-escalation of the conflict with Iran are encouraging, but may be premature, as tensions remain high and navigation in the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted. Forex.com market analyst Fawad Razaqzada stated, "We've seen crude oil prices pull back, but Brent crude futures are holding above $100 a barrel, indicating the market isn't fully convinced. Without a clearer timetable for reopening, and more importantly, a lack of credibility, it's difficult to see oil prices sustainably falling below $100." (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 68,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.98% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 01, 2026, 13:48 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 68,000 USDT and is now trading at 67,994.007813 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.98% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: US Manufacturing Shows Resilience Amid Middle East Conflict, Expert Says
data.description: According to Jin10, Chris Williamson, Chief Business Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, commented on the US March PMI data, noting that the US manufacturing sector has demonstrated encouraging resilience amid the outbreak of war in the Middle East. Business confidence in future output remains strong so far. This ongoing resilience is partly due to reduced concerns over government policies and suggests that manufacturers expect the impact of the war to be short-term and limited, though uncertainty persists. Currently, the conflict's impact is in its early stages, with sharp price increases and delivery delays casting a shadow over the outlook, potentially driving inflation, suppressing demand, and disrupting supply chains. Driven by soaring oil prices, factory input costs have risen significantly, and supplier delays are more common than at any time since October 2022. As a result, some manufacturers are increasing inventories to prepare for future price hikes or supply shortages, while hiring has nearly stalled to reduce labor costs, highlighting concerns about potential issues the war may cause for factories in the coming weeks.

data.title: Tally Announces Transition in Governance App Development
data.description: Tally has announced that its long-term partner, ScopeLift, will continue to oversee the development of the Tally Governance App during the transition period. According to Foresight News, services for enterprise clients and licensees will remain unaffected. The Governance App codebase is set to transition to an MIT license in 12 months, allowing community access.

data.title: U.S. March S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Finalized at 52.3
data.description: The final reading for the U.S. S&P Global Manufacturing PMI in March was recorded at 52.3. According to Odaily, this figure shows a slight decrease from the previous value of 52.4. The PMI is a key indicator of the economic health of the manufacturing sector, with values above 50 indicating expansion.

data.title: The final reading of the S&P Global Manufacturing PMI for March in the United States was 52.3, compared to 52.4 in the previous month.
data.description: The final reading of the US S&P Global Manufacturing PMI for March was 52.3, compared to 52.4 in the previous month. (Jinshi)

data.title: CoinDesk 20 Sees Slight Increase as Majority of Assets Trade Higher
data.description: CoinDesk 20 experienced a 1.0% rise, reaching 1,968.28 since the previous day's close. According to NS3.AI, eighteen out of the twenty assets within the index showed positive trading activity, contributing to the overall increase.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Moussalon: The energy shock from the Iran war will push up inflation across the board.
data.description: St. Louis Federal Reserve President Mohamed Moussalem said on Wednesday that the energy shock related to the war with Iran will certainly affect overall inflation and could impact core price pressures. Speaking at an event hosted by the American Enterprise Institute in Washington, Moussalem said, "I expect commodity price increases to have a one-to-one impact on overall inflation, and we will see a period of high overall inflation." He added, "Core inflation will also be affected to some extent, and I will closely monitor the extent of the transmission and the correlation between the two." (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Dips Below 68000 USDT
data.description: Bitcoin's price has fallen below 68000 USDT, according to Odaily. The current price is reported at 67967.8 USDT, with the 24-hour growth rate narrowing to 1.28%.

data.title: Launch of Tokenized High-Yield Bond Fund on Ethereum by OpenEden and BNY Investments
data.description: OpenEden and BNY Investments have introduced HYBOND, a tokenized version of OpenEden's Global Short-Dated High-Yield Bond Fund, on the Ethereum blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this innovative financial product is initially accessible to select institutional clients. A wider institutional rollout is anticipated by the end of April 2026, with plans for future expansion to the XRP Ledger and BNB Chain.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Market Opens Higher as Nike Shares Plummet
data.description: U.S. stock markets opened higher with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising by 0.57%, the S&P 500 increasing by 0.66%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbing 0.7%. According to Jin10, Nike's shares fell sharply by 12% following the company's unexpected revenue decline forecast for the current quarter. In the energy sector, ExxonMobil and Chevron saw declines of 1.8% and 1.7%, respectively. Meanwhile, Intel's stock rose approximately 3% as the company announced plans to repurchase a 49% stake in its wafer manufacturing joint venture in Ireland.

data.title: Federal Reserve Official Compares Current Inflation to 1970s Levels
data.description: Federal Reserve official Musalem has stated that current inflation indicators are better than those of the 1970s. According to ChainCatcher, Musalem's comments highlight a more favorable economic situation compared to the high inflation experienced during that decade. The statement reflects ongoing assessments of inflation trends and their implications for economic policy.

data.title: Federal Reserve Official Commits to Reducing Inflation to 2%
data.description: Federal Reserve official Musalem has expressed his commitment to lowering inflation to 2%. According to ChainCatcher, Musalem emphasized the importance of achieving this target to ensure economic stability. His remarks come amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy and its impact on inflation rates. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor economic indicators closely as it navigates the challenges of maintaining price stability.
